description_long: This is a large, industrial-grade control cabinet typically found in urban infrastructure settings such as streets, substations, or utility areas. The cabinet is composed of two main vertical compartments with metallic doors, each featuring a black top section that likely houses ventilation or cooling units. The left compartment has a control panel with several colored buttons and indicator lights, as well as a digital display, suggesting it is used for monitoring and controlling electrical systems. There are visible ventilation grilles on the top, and the cabinet is equipped with robust handles and possible locking mechanisms for security. The exterior is primarily a matte gray metal, with some black and colored accents, and there are several labels and a manufacturer plate affixed to the front. The overall construction appears sturdy and weather-resistant, suitable for outdoor or semi-outdoor installation.
description: A large, dual-compartment metal control cabinet with a control panel, ventilation grilles, and security handles, designed for urban utility management. The exterior is gray with black tops and colored indicator lights.
description_view_0: Front view showing two main doors, a control panel with buttons and lights, ventilation grilles on top, and visible labels.
description_view_1: Side view showing the depth of the cabinet and the overhanging black top sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, solid back with the black top sections and no visible controls or labels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the cabinet's depth and the black top sections.
